Politics.headline: Report slams government's pay equity reforms as 'secretive, 'mischievous' and negotiated in bad faith
Politics.detail: A report released by “The People's Select Committee” condemns the government's pay equity reforms last year, citing a lack of consultation and the extinguishing of 33 existing pay equity claims, which saved the government $12.8 billion. The committee is calling for the repeal of last year's changes and the creation of a government-funded pay equity unit to help assess claims.

Politics.headline: Minister Anand to participate in a fireside chat marking four years since Russia’s full‑scale invasion of Ukraine
Politics.detail: The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, will deliver a speech and participate in a fireside chat to mark four years since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, and will be joined by Marie-Eve Sylvestre, President and Vice-Chancellor of the University of Ottawa, and Roland Paris, Director of the Graduate School of Public International Affairs. The event will take place on February 24, 2026, at 12:30 EST in Ottawa, Ontario.

Politics.headline: Department of National Defence launching next phase of residential housing construction program
Politics.detail: The Honourable David J. McGuinty, Minister of National Defence, will host a news conference on February 24, 2026, in Ottawa, ON, regarding the next phase of the Department of National Defence's housing program, which will expand military housing for CAF members and their families across Canada. Interested media must confirm their attendance with National Defence's Media Relations Office by email no later than 7 a.m. EDT on February 24, 2026.

Politics.headline: Ask the editor: Kathleen Hennessey on local news and what's next for the Minnesota Star Tribune
Politics.detail: MPR News host Angela Davis will be talking with Kathleen Hennessey, editor and senior vice president of the Minnesota Star Tribune, about how newsrooms are trying to adapt to changes in news consumption. The discussion will also cover how the Star Tribune decides what to cover, how well it covers issues in the community, and the future of local news.

Politics.headline: British police arrest Epstein-linked ex-ambassador Mandelson
Politics.detail: Peter Mandelson, a former British ambassador to the US, was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office after allegations surfaced that he sent confidential documents to Jeffrey Epstein while in government. He has been bailed after the arrest.

Finance & Economy.headline: Bank of Korea to keep interest rates steady at 2.50% through 2026: Reuters poll
Finance & Economy.detail: South Korea's central bank is expected to maintain its key interest rate at 2.50% throughout 2026, amidst concerns about currency volatility and an overheating housing market. Economists foresee no policy changes, prioritizing exchange rate stability despite inflation easing to 2.0% in January.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks drop after Trump ramps up his tariffs and investors dump potential AI losers
Finance & Economy.detail: US stocks experienced a decline following President Trump's decision to increase tariffs, coupled with investors selling off stocks of companies perceived as potential losers in the AI sector. This reflects market sensitivity to trade policy changes and evolving views on the AI industry's impact.

Finance & Economy.headline: Economists expect upbeat 2026 Budget speech, US producer price index in focus
Finance & Economy.detail: Economists anticipate a positive 2026 national Budget speech from South Africa's Finance minister Enoch Godongwana, supported by high commodity prices. Globally, the US producer price index is expected to provide insights into inflationary pressures in the world's largest economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump eyes new trade probes to revive tariffs after court defeat
Finance & Economy.detail: Following a court defeat, President Trump is considering initiating new trade investigations to reinstate tariffs. This move signals a continued focus on trade as an economic policy tool despite legal challenges.

Finance & Economy.headline: Financial Resilience in 2026: Navigating Inflation and Tax Complexity
Finance & Economy.detail: In 2026, protecting capital requires analytical insight and management due to market instability, inflation, and evolving tax rules in the UK. Fidenti Wealth emphasizes transparency, advanced technology, and tax efficiency to safeguard wealth.

Technology.headline: Fujitsu expands Uvance for Finance offerings to accelerate DX across financial sector
Technology.detail: Fujitsu is expanding and reorganizing its Uvance for Finance offerings to cover the entire financial sector and accelerate digital transformation by leveraging data and AI. The enhanced offerings are structured into seven new offerings and aim to achieve 200 billion yen in sales by fiscal year 2030.

Technology.headline: AI Rapidly Rendering Cyber Defenses Obsolete: Report
Technology.detail: AI adoption is outpacing enterprise security, leaving organizations exposed to machine-speed attacks and unmanaged AI risk. Sensitive corporate data is increasingly flowing through unsanctioned GenAI apps as workers bypass official controls, widening the security risk.

Technology.headline: Latest Tech & Open Source News: Your Innovation Hub
Technology.detail: This article discusses the latest tech and open source news, highlighting how open source AI frameworks are accelerating research in fields like medicine and environmental science. It emphasizes the collaborative spirit and rapid integration of new features driven by real-world user needs.

Technology.headline: Apple plans to manufacture Mac Mini in Houston, WSJ reports
Technology.detail: Apple will move some production of its Mac Mini desktop computer to the U.S. from Asia, with manufacturing beginning later this year at a Foxconn facility in north Houston. This effort was confirmed by Apple's chief operating officer, Sabih Khan, to the WSJ.

Technology.headline: Who is Asha Sharma? A closer look at Microsoft's surprise pick to lead the Xbox business
Technology.detail: Microsoft's new top gaming exec, Asha Sharma, has the trust of Satya Nadella and a track record running platforms at Facebook, Instacart, and Microsoft's AI division. Her goal is to restore Xbox's "renegade spirit," though she lacks gaming industry experience, which the community is watching closely.

Science.headline: Not just drainage: Dural venous sinuses actively regulate brain immunity and fluid flow, study finds
Science.detail: A recent study has found that dural venous sinuses actively regulate brain immunity and fluid flow, going beyond their known function of just draining blood from the brain. This discovery enhances the understanding of brain health and potential therapeutic interventions.

Science.headline: Smarter tissue and organ repair thanks to next-gen hydrogel
Science.detail: A new generation of hydrogel is enabling smarter tissue and organ repair. This could lead to more effective treatments and regenerative medicine.

Science.headline: Astronomers discover rare super-Jupiter orbiting distant star
Science.detail: Astronomers have recently discovered a rare super-Jupiter orbiting a distant star. This finding provides valuable insights into the formation and evolution of planetary systems.

Science.headline: Cleaner wrasse have revealed a remarkable new side of fish intelligence
Science.detail: Cleaner wrasse demonstrate advanced cognitive abilities by using mirrors to inspect and remove fake parasites, showcasing a new dimension of fish intelligence. These fish quickly learned to use the mirrors to spot and remove the marked spots.

Science.headline: Far beyond Neptune, in the frozen depths of the Kuiper Belt, many ancient objects oddly resemble giant snowmen made of ice and rock
Science.detail: Scientists are investigating how the peculiar two-lobed shapes of ancient objects in the Kuiper Belt, resembling giant snowmen, could have formed without destructive collisions. These objects present a mystery in planetary formation.

Health.headline: Tens of thousands with vitiligo to be offered “life-changing” new cream on NHS to restore skin colour
Health.detail: Nearly 100,000 people aged 12 and over with vitiligo affecting their face will be offered ruxolitinib cream on the NHS, the first approved medicine to restore lost skin color, after standard steroid creams have not worked. The treatment, applied twice daily, has shown significant improvements in facial re-pigmentation in clinical trials, offering hope for those emotionally distressed by the condition.

Health.headline: [CROI 2026] AJMC: The 4 I's: How HIV prevention, treatment can move forward in challenging times
Health.detail: At CROI 2026, Ilesh Jani, PhD, outlined a plan emphasizing 4 I's for advancing HIV treatment and prevention, in light of budget cuts and halted research. The lecture reviewed progress made in HIV over the past 30 years and addressed the challenges the past year has brought.

Health.headline: Putting Women's Health First: Ovarian Cancer Awareness Month
Health.detail: February is Ovarian Cancer Awareness Month, highlighting the need for awareness and timely medical review due to the subtle and non-specific symptoms of ovarian cancer. Advances in targeted therapies and personalized treatment are offering hope for women, even when the cancer cannot be cured.

Health.headline: The science of Ramadan fasting
Health.detail: UC Riverside reports on the science of Ramadan fasting, noting that while it can lead to modest short-term improvements in blood pressure and lipid markers, long-term benefits depend on sustained healthy behaviors. The article addresses who should avoid fasting, such as those with uncontrolled diabetes or advanced heart disease, without medical clearance.

Health.headline: Federal government seeking input to develop men's and boys' health strategy
Health.detail: The Canadian federal government is initiating a national conversation to gather input for a future health strategy focused on men and boys. This signifies a move towards addressing specific health needs and challenges faced by this demographic.

Sports.headline: Hughes scores in overtime as US beats Canada for first men's hockey gold at the Olympics since 1980
Sports.detail: The United States defeated Canada in overtime to win the men's hockey gold medal at the Winter Olympics, marking their first victory since the 'Miracle on Ice' in 1980. Jack Hughes scored the winning goal in the thrilling final.

Sports.headline: Twin Milan and Cortina Olympic cauldrons are extinguished, signaling the end of Winter Games
Sports.detail: The 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan and Cortina, Italy, have come to a close, with the extinguishing of the twin Olympic cauldrons. Norway finished as the highest-ranked team in the medal table.

Sports.headline: WNBA says March 10 deadline needed for new CBA to avoid delaying May 8 season start, AP source says
Sports.detail: The WNBA has set a deadline of March 10 for a new Collective Bargaining Agreement to be reached in order to avoid any delays to the start of the season on May 8. An AP source confirmed the importance of this deadline.

Sports.headline: Kara Braxton, former WNBA All-Star and 2-time champion, dies at 43
Sports.detail: Former WNBA All-Star and two-time champion, Kara Braxton, has passed away at the age of 43. Braxton was also the mother of Ohio State Buckeyes tight end Jelani Thurman.

Sports.headline: Lindsey Vonn says surgery saved her from having her left leg amputated following Olympic crash
Sports.detail: Lindsey Vonn revealed that she almost had her left leg amputated following a crash during the Olympics, but surgery saved it. She also needed a blood transfusion.

Entertainment.headline: Nick Reiner Pleads Not Guilty to Murder
Entertainment.detail: Nick Reiner, the son of Rob Reiner, pleaded not guilty in Los Angeles to two counts of murder in the killing of his parents, Rob Reiner and Michele Singer Reiner. The 32-year-old faces charges with the special circumstance of multiple murders.

Entertainment.headline: BAFTA and BBC apologize for racial slur during awards show
Entertainment.detail: The British Academy Film Awards and BBC have apologized for a racial slur that was broadcast during Sunday's show while two stars of the film “Sinners” were onstage. The apology was issued on Monday.

Entertainment.headline: Willie Colón, architect of urban salsa music, dies at 75
Entertainment.detail: Willie Colón, a significant figure in urban salsa music, has passed away at the age of 75. Colón was a singer and musician.

Entertainment.headline: 'One Battle After Another': Leonardo DiCaprio's Dystopian Movie Release Date, Plot, Cast & More
Entertainment.detail: Leonardo DiCaprio stars in the action thriller 'One Battle After Another' as a former revolutionary tasked with rescuing his daughter, set in an authoritarian version of America. The film details the challenges he faces in this mission.

Entertainment.headline: ‘The Jeffersons’ star Marla Gibbs reveals she beat brain aneurysm that ‘most people do not survive’
Entertainment.detail: Marla Gibbs, known for her role in 'The Jeffersons,' disclosed that she survived a brain aneurysm, a condition that is often fatal. She shared this information recently.

Crime & Justice.headline: Rob Reiner's son pleads not guilty to 2 counts of first-degree murder in the killing of his parents
Crime & Justice.detail: Nick Reiner, the 32-year-old son of Rob Reiner and Michele Singer Reiner, pleaded not guilty Monday to two counts of first-degree murder more than two months after their deaths.

Crime & Justice.headline: An attacker detonates an explosive device in Moscow, killing a police officer and himself
Crime & Justice.detail: Russian officials say an unidentified assailant has detonated an explosive device next to a patrol vehicle in Moscow, killing himself and a police officer and leaving two other officers injured.

Crime & Justice.headline: Shiprock Man Sentenced to Prison for Assaulting Two Men with a Knife
Crime & Justice.detail: A Shiprock man was sentenced to prison for assaulting two men with a knife.

Crime & Justice.headline: Duke of Marlborough pleads not guilty to strangulation charges.
Crime & Justice.detail: The Duke of Marlborough has pleaded not guilty to strangulation charges.

Crime & Justice.headline: New details emerge about armed man shot and killed at Trump's Mar-a-Lago
Crime & Justice.detail: New details have emerged regarding the armed man who was shot and killed at Trump's Mar-a-Lago property.

Education.headline: Trump administration steps up efforts to scrutinize foreign funding of universities
Education.detail: The Trump administration is increasing efforts to uncover foreign influence at US colleges and universities, with the State Department assisting the Department of Education in this endeavor. President Trump has threatened to cut federal funding to universities over issues like pro-Palestinian protests, transgender policies, and DEI programs.

Education.headline: UAE announces 2026-2029 school calendar dates
Education.detail: The UAE Government has announced the school calendar dates for 2026-2029, including start and end dates for academic years, as well as mid-term, winter, and spring breaks. This year, a federal decree law on the governance of the National Educational Curriculum was also announced, which is the first comprehensive legislative framework regulating the design, approval, implementation, and review of the curriculum in the UAE.

Education.headline: Rosalind Smith honoured as trailblazer in Edmonton's public school system
Education.detail: In 1996, Rosalind Smith made history as the first Black principal for the Edmonton Public School Board. She is being honored as a trailblazer in Edmonton's public school system.

Education.headline: Home-country study clause resurfaces as Australia tightens study visa scrutiny
Education.detail: More South Asian applicants, especially from Sri Lanka, are being refused Australian study visas because similar programs exist in their home country. Experts say this is an old reason and may be part of a wider effort to slow international student growth after recent expansion.

Education.headline: The Guardian view on Send reforms: ministers need to show how inclusion will work.
Education.detail: Changes to the Send system in England were discussed, as well as the impact of those changes on children. Parents of children with Send gave the changes in England a mixed response.

Lifestyle.headline: Six underrated – and affordable – shower upgrades, including a better bath towel and cleaner water filter
Lifestyle.detail: Small changes to your shower routine and setup can significantly improve your daily experience without breaking the bank, from better towels to water filters.

Lifestyle.headline: Quote of the day by Rosa Parks: 'I have learned over the years that when one's…'
Lifestyle.detail: A quote from Rosa Parks is featured, offering inspiration and reflection.

Lifestyle.headline: Researchers find TikTok fitness videos cause body image issues
Lifestyle.detail: A study has revealed that fitness-related content on TikTok can contribute to negative body image perceptions.

Lifestyle.headline: Tim Tebow tells story of Jesus through eyes of the cross in revealing new book
Lifestyle.detail: Tim Tebow's new book "If the Tree Could Speak" tells the crucifixion story of Jesus from a tree's perspective, publishing Feb. 24 with a unique take on Christian faith.

Lifestyle.headline: Our staff-favorite recipes for quick, low-effort meals
Lifestyle.detail: The Washington Post shares staff-approved recipes for tasty and quick meals when energy levels are low.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Sovereignty set in concrete
Opinion/Editorial.detail: There is more than one way to assert sovereignty beyond diplomatic protests and verbal barbs; for the government, the most effective one yet may not require words at all but something literally concrete–runways and ports that can withstand monsoon seas and foreign aggression in equal measure.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the fourth anniversary of Putin's war: Ukraine is exhausted, but not broken
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial focuses on the state of Ukraine four years after the start of the war with Russia.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Adjusting to US ruling on tariffs
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US' highest court on Friday struck down a number of the White House's tariffs in a revision of the extent and form of power wielded by US President Donald Trump's administration over foreign trade and economic relations.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A deal that settles nothing
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial discussing a deal that is perceived to not resolve underlying issues.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Democratic Party's 'Hyunjih Nuna' Spokesperson: No Checks
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Democratic Party appointed former Cheong Wa Dae Digital Communication Secretary Kim Nam-kuk as its new spokesperson, approximately two months after he was effectively dismissed from Cheong Wa Dae due to the 'Hyunjih Nuna' personnel solicitation controversy. This appointment highlights the party's lax oversight amid election preparations.

World.headline: Mexico just decapitated its most dangerous cartel. That means war.
World.detail: President Claudia Sheinbaum needs to stay the course after the killing of the leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

World.headline: Heavy rains, deadly floods hit southern Peru; thousands seek shelter
World.detail: Torrential downpours cause deadly mudslides, while more than 700 districts across the country declare states of emergency.

World.headline: France blocks US ambassador's access to ministers after he fails to show for meeting
World.detail: The French government has reportedly blocked the US ambassador's access to ministers after he did not attend a meeting.

World.headline: Security forces keep up fight with cartel gunmen a day after the Mexican military killed a drug lord
World.detail: School was canceled in several Mexican states, and local and foreign governments alike warned their citizens to stay inside following the army's killing, and the violence it spurred.

World.headline: EU fails to agree on new sanctions on Russia ahead of fourth anniversary of war – Europe live
World.detail: The European Union has been unable to agree on new sanctions against Russia as the fourth anniversary of the war in Ukraine approaches.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chatunga Mugabe to stay in jail until at least March 3 after court hearing
Zimbabwe.detail: The son of the late former President Robert Mugabe, Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, will remain in custody until at least March 3 after a court hearing in Johannesburg, where he faces charges of attempted murder, illegal possession of a firearm, and defeating the ends of justice related to a shooting at his home. Prosecutors read out the charges against him.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu PF Harare spokesperson wanted in South Africa after skipping bail
Zimbabwe.detail: Joachim Chivayo, the Zanu PF Harare spokesperson, is wanted in South Africa after fleeing to Zimbabwe in connection with a R15 million illegal gold dealing case. He skipped bail and is now being sought by South African authorities.

Zimbabwe.headline: High Court quashes 'King Munhumutapa' conviction for undermining Mnangagwa
Zimbabwe.detail: The High Court has overturned the conviction of a self-styled king, King Munhumutapa, who had been jailed for running 'parallel structures' and undermining President Mnangagwa. The conviction was quashed, freeing him from jail.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe wins toss and opts to bowl against West Indies in Super 8s at the T20 World Cup
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe won the toss and chose to bowl against the West Indies in their Super 8s Group 1 match at the Twenty20 World Cup. The match took place in Mumbai, India, and both teams made one change to their lineups.

Zimbabwe.headline: Independent Apologises for 'Poorly Executed Hatchet Job' On ZBC
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Independent has issued an unreserved apology to President Mnangagwa, the Ministry of Information, Publicity and Broadcasting Services, and the Zimbabwe Broadcasting Corporation (ZBC) for publishing false allegations regarding missing licence-fee revenues. The publication admitted that its story suggesting US$52 million was unaccounted for at ZBC was not supported by verified official findings.
